Brooklyn’s Finest (2010)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $27,163,593Details
International Box Office $12,069,640
Worldwide Box Office $39,233,233
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $11,021,369
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,892,286
Total $14,913,655
Further financial details...

Synopsis

When NYPD's Operation Clean Up targets the notoriously drug-ridden BK housing project, three conflicted New York City police officers find themselves swept away by the violence and corruption of Brooklyn's gritty 65th Precinct and its most treacherous criminals. Each man finds himself spiraling toward the same fatal crime scene and a shattering collision with destiny.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$13,350,299 (49.1% of total gross)
Legs:2.03 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:69.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$17,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:1,936 opening theaters/1,939 max. theaters, 3.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $41,175,733

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 5th, 2010 (Wide) by Overture Films
International Releases: September 27th, 2012 (Wide), released as Brooklyn's Finest (Bolivia)
Video Release: July 6th, 2010 by Overture Films/Anchor Bay
MPA Rating: R for bloody violence throughout, strong sexuality, nudity, drug content and pervasive language.
Running Time: 133 minutes
Plot point: Corrupt Cops, Mistaken Identity, Money Troubles, NYPD, One Last Job, Undercover
Social setting: Hood Film, Retirement
Subgenre: Crime Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Millennium Films, Thunder Road Film, Nu Image, Langley Films Inc
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

When NYPD's Operation Clean Up targets the notoriously drug-ridden BK housing project, three conflicted New York City police officers find themselves swept away by the violence and corruption of Brooklyn's gritty 65th Precinct and its most treacherous criminals. Each man finds himself spiraling toward the same fatal crime scene and a shattering collision with destiny.
